div.p: themselves, and they are their own accusers. “But,” says one, “I believe in one universal heaven and hell, where all go, and are all alike, and equally miserable or equally happy.”

What! where all are huddled together—the honorable, virtuous, and murderers, and whoremongers, when it is written that they shall be judged according to the deeds done in the body?1 But St. Paul informs us of three glories and three heavens.2 He knew a man that was caught up to the third heavens.3 Now, if the doctrine of the sectarian world, that there is but one heaven, is true, Paul, what do you tell that lie for, and say there are three? Jesus said unto His disciples, “In my Father’s house are many mansions, if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you, and I will come and receive you to myself, that where I am ye may be also.”4
div.div: Men of God Endowed with Wisdom
div.p: Any man may believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God,5 and be happy in that belief, and yet not obey his commandments,6 and at last be cut down for disobedience to the Lord’s righteous requirements.

A man of God7 should be endowed with wisdom, knowledge,8 and understanding, in order to teach and lead the people of God.9 The sectarian priests are blind, and they lead the blind, and they will all fall into the ditch together.10 They build with hay, wood, and stubble,11 on the old revelations, without the true priesthood or spirit of revelation. If I had time, I would dig into hell, hades, sheol, and tell what exists there.

There is much said about God and the Godhead.12 The scriptures say there are Gods many and Lords many, but to us there is but one living and true God,13 and the heaven of heavens14 could not contain him;15 for he took the liberty to go into other heavens. The teachers of the day say that the Father is God, the Son is God, and the Holy Ghost is God, and they are all in one body and one God.16 Jesus prayed that those that the Father had given him out of the world might be made one in them, as they were one [one in spirit, in mind, in purpose].17 If I were to testify that the Christian world were wrong on this point, my testimony would be true.18
